config.seed_text: A forgotten machine beneath the sea continued speaking long after its makers were gone. Each repetition made its message stranger, denser, and more self-aware.

model.notes: Fastest baseline; useful as a collapse-prone small reference model.

records.reflection_text: The best solution was to write what it called an automated version of one or more words into each sentence with no need for rewrites—or any further changes without knowing where they came from; which is why you'll never see anything like this again! Even when your sentences start getting repetitive even before them become so fast (you're probably not going anywhere if both ends result). If someone takes longer than 1 second on every word then read their entire history but don't forget about "words" by just copying over old ones as well...and repeat these countless times until we finally learn how many people really understood our idea right away…it won´t be possible - yet another way to improve upon existing understanding/experience while maintaining some kind sort 'nonsense' concept can still remain alive at
